The nature of the claim:

TRIP AND FALL, to cover money damages for personal injury, pain and suffering, medical expenses and related damages incurred by and on behalf of claimant REBECA FLORES MAYA, by reason of the negligence, recklessness and carelessness, negligent hiring and supervision of said agents, intentional and careless action of THE CITY OF NEW YORK and NYC PARKS AUTHORITY.

From sample 2:
2. The nature of the claim: Personal Injury Action to recover for serious and permanent personal injuries, bodily injury, physical injury, conscious pain and suffering, future pain and suffering, medical expenses, future medical expenses, lost earnings, lost earnings capacity and capability, loss of services, society, consortium and affection due to the negligence, recklessness and carelessness of the CITY OF NEW YORK (collectively hereinafter as “respondent”).

From sample 1:
Time, place and manner in which claim arose:
On May 28, 2025, at or about [???] on a sidewalk located at 4047 Broadway, County, City and State of New York. Claimant was at the aforementioned sidewalk/premises when she was trip and fell due to due to an uneven surface and sidewalk defect. 

That THE CITY OF NEW YORK and NYC PARKS AUTHORITY, their agents, servants and/or employees were careless, reckless and negligent in the operation, maintenance and control of the aforesaid sidewalk and more particularly the steps at 4047 Broadway, County, City and State of New York, there at, Claimant was caused to trip and fell due to a uneven, defective, dangerous and hazardous steps at the aforesaid location and unsafe for pedestrians; in violating their duties to the general public and in causing and/or permitting the aforementioned conditions to exist for a considerable length of time prior to the accident, and THE CITY OF NEW YORK and NYC PARKS AUTHORITY, their agents, servants and/or employees knew or should have known that such hazardous condition would cause persons to trip and fall, and more particularly the plaintiff herein; in failing to maintain said sidewalk in a reasonable and safe for pedestrians; in allowing the said steps to remain uneven, defective, faulty, unsafe, hazardous and otherwise dangerous sidewalk located at 4047 Broadway, County, City and State of New York, in failing to correct or remedy such conditions all of which were actively known; in failing to inspect said sidewalk; in failing to warn of the danger of said defective and dangerous condition; in allowing a dangerous condition to exist causing a hazard to the life and limbs of plaintiff; in failing to use reasonable care, caution and forbearance that should have been exercised under the circumstances and the situation that prevailed at the time and place of said occurrence; in violating the laws of the State of New York and the rules, regulations and ordinances of the City of New York in force and effect at the time of the happening of this accident; all these conditions THE CITY OF NEW YORK and NYC PARKS AUTHORITY, their agents, servants and/or employees had due notice of or by reasonable care and inspection could have avoided the same; THE CITY OF NEW YORK and NYC PARKS AUTHORITY, their agents, servants and/or employees were careless, all of which was the direct, proximate and/or concurrent cause of the injuries sustained by plaintiff without any negligence of the plaintiff contributing thereto.

Claimant is claiming both actual and constructive notice. Actual notice is claimed in that THE CITY OF NEW YORK and NYC PARKS AUTHORITY, by their agents, servants and/or employees caused, created, permitted, allowed, observed and/or were aware that said condition existed prior to plaintiff’s trip and fall. Claimant reserves her right to supplement/amend this response up to the time of trial.

Constructive notice is claimed on the basis that the condition existed for an unreasonable length of time, such that in the exercise of reasonable care and in the inspection of the sidewalk of the afore-mentioned location, THE CITY OF NEW YORK and NYC PARKS AUTHORITY created the condition. Further details of constructive notice will be supplied should they become available upon completion of discovery.

From sample 2:
3. The time when, the place where and the manner in which the claim arose: The incident occurred at or about 1:00 PM on NOVEMBER 2, 2025, on the sidewalk abutting located at 349 EAST 51ST STREET, NEW YORK, NEW YORK 10022. Attached is a color photograph of the subject sidewalk. 

At that time and place, claimant was lawfully walking on the subject sidewalk, the claimant caused to trip and fall due to uneven, defective and raised portion of the sidewalk at the subject location. Due to the dangerous nature of the subject sidewalk, the claimant suffered serious personal injuries. 

The injuries sustained was a result of the negligence, recklessness and carelessness of the respondent in its ownership, maintenance, supervision, inspection, repair, use, special use and management of said sidewalk. Respondent was negligent, reckless and careless in its ownership, operation, management, maintenance, repair, inspection, use, utilization, occupying of, possession and/or supervision of the subject sidewalk. Respondent also failed to warn of the subject dangerous condition and barricade same. Respondent failed to have the sidewalk in question up to all building, local, commercial, county, state, village, state, construction, safety, fire and/or other codes; in allowing, permitting, causing and/or creating a dangerous condition to occur, exist and remain; in failing to be in compliance with the applicable codes of such jurisdictions; failed to remedy the aforementioned sidewalk and condition; failed to barricade the dangerous condition and sidewalk; failed to repair the subject sidewalk; failed to warn of the dangerous condition; failed to barricade the subject dangerous condition and sidewalk; in failing to properly maintain, inspect, repair, supervise, manage and/or remedy the subject sidewalk and condition; by allowing the defective condition to exist for a lengthy period of time prior to CLAIMANT’S occurrence;  failure to inspect the subject sidewalk to ensure the sidewalk in question was kept in a proper and safe condition; in creating the dangerous condition; failed to barricade such sidewalk and condition so that pedestrians would not encounter it; in failing to remedy the defective, uneven, dangerous and hazardous condition which they had previous notice of; failed to properly maintain the subject sidewalk; failed to properly monitor the subject sidewalk; and to be otherwise negligent.

Respondent, its agents, servants and/or employees, was careless and negligent, in among other things, its failure to properly maintain, control, inspect, repair, remedy and manage the subject sidewalk; in failing to fix the aforementioned defective, uneven, dangerous condition; in failing to remedy the defective, uneven, hazardous and dangerous condition after such condition existed for an unreasonable period of time; in failing to keep the aforementioned sidewalk clear of dangerous and hazardous conditions; in its failure to properly maintain property owned and maintained by Respondent.  It is alleged that Respondent and/or third parties caused and created the defective, uneven, dangerous condition. 

All respondent had actual, constructive, and prior written notice of the condition that caused CLAIMANT’S fall. Respondent also had constructive notice of the subject condition that caused CLAIMANT’S accident. CLAIMANT alleges compliance with all applicable statutory notice laws. Respondent had notice of the subject condition by way of records kept, generated, maintained and created, amongst other notice given.

It is alleged that respondent had prior written notice of the aforesaid condition that caused this occurrence. It is also alleged, upon information and belief, that respondent: affirmatively created the subject condition through its negligent maintenance and/or repair and/or replacement of the subject sidewalk/area. It is also alleged that respondent had actual and constructive notice of said dangerous condition prior to the occurrence. It is alleged that respondent created the dangerous condition that caused claimant’s occurrence.

It is alleged that respondent had prior written notice of the aforesaid condition that caused this occurrence. It is also alleged, upon information and belief, that respondent: affirmatively created the subject condition through its negligent maintenance and/or repair and/or replacement of the subject sidewalk, including but not limited to performing work in the subject sidewalk, utility work, water utility work, water utility function, gas function, gas utility work and/or sidewalk work resulting in a dangerous condition to form through its affirmative act(s) of negligence. It is also alleged that respondent made special use of the subject sidewalk for its own benefit, including that municipal repairs, and maintenance for respondent’s benefit and/or other sidewalk maintenance. It is also claimed that respondent created the dangerous condition through the affirmative acts of negligence of respondent; It is also alleged that respondent had actual and constructive notice of said dangerous condition prior to the occurrence. It is also alleged that respondent had performed utility maintenance and/or sidewalk work in the subject sidewalk, which created and/or affirmatively created the subject condition and/or the negligent maintenance and/or negligent repair through affirmative acts of negligence. It is alleged that respondent created the dangerous condition that caused claimant’s occurrence. It is also alleged that respondent made special use of the subject sidewalk and conferred a special benefit upon the municipality, including but limited to benefits of sidewalk functions, amongst other benefits to respondent. Respondent’s affirmative acts of negligence include that maintenance and/or repair of the subject sidewalk.
